steps.title: Track Ulfheim's praise
steps.content: The underlined words are Ulfheim's formal address to Professor Rubek: he says it is an honor to meet "no less a person than the great Sculptor Rubek." That wording functions as praise, not as a detail about when they met.

steps.title: Separate praise from details
steps.content: A concerns the later statement about meeting years ago, not the underlined address. C invents a preference for a season. D reverses the passage's praise by suggesting many sculptors outrank Rubek. The answer is B.
